text.title: MLP Planning Examples
text.summary: Note-taking app and invoicing tool MVP-to-MLP transformations with before/after tables, layer-by-layer improvements, and retention outcomes.

example.note: MVP state: create, edit, delete notes; basic formatting; functional but bland. Day-30 retention: 18%. NPS: 30.
Layer 2 (Reliable): added auto-save with visual confirmation. Eliminated "did it save?" anxiety.
Layer 3 (Usable): added keyboard shortcuts for common actions; quick capture from any screen.
Layer 4 (Delight/Speed): smooth animations on state transitions; sub-50ms search.
Layer 4 (Delight/Personality): "Zen mode" for focused writing; clever empty states with contextual prompts.
Layer 4 (Delight/Celebration): satisfying sound on note save (opt-in).
Result: Day-30 retention increased 40% (from 18% to 25%); NPS from 30 to 55. Core action time decreased 30%.
MVP state: create invoices, send to clients. Functional but clunky. Core action (send invoice) takes 4 minutes. Day-30 retention: 35%.
Layer 2 (Reliable): payment confirmation notifications with push and email fallback.
Layer 3 (Usable): templates pre-filled from past invoices; client auto-fill from contact database.
Layer 4 (Delight/Celebration): animated celebration screen on payment received with amount summary.
Layer 4 (Delight/Anticipation): "You've earned $X this month" dashboard tile; proactive invoice reminder suggestions.
Result: users create 2x more invoices per month; NPS 60; core action time reduced from 4 minutes to 90 seconds.

antipattern.description: Adding more features to an MVP instead of polishing existing ones to MLP threshold.
antipattern.reason: Feature addition expands surface area for friction without addressing the root retention problem. Most MVPs reach MLP by improving the core 3 features to Layer 4, not by adding a 4th feature.

antipattern.description: Surface-level polish (new colors, marketing copy) while ignoring interaction quality (latency, defaults, error recovery).
antipattern.reason: Visual polish is visible but does not compound; interaction quality (speed, anticipation, recovery) affects every session and compounds with usage frequency.

antipattern.description: Skipping numeric MLP completion criteria before starting delight work.
antipattern.reason: Without exit criteria, "MLP" expands indefinitely — teams keep polishing without shipping. The criteria are the commitment mechanism that ends the sprint.

antipattern.description: Generic delight (confetti everywhere) not tailored to the user persona.
antipattern.reason: B2B enterprise users find confetti unprofessional; Japanese users find animated celebrations intrusive. Delight must be calibrated to the specific persona's emotional context.
